_comment: BloomLeedz deployment manifest — Bloomsights K-12 outreach to 318 school principals/admins in Los Angeles.

deployment.name: BloomLeedz
deployment._dbFile_note: DUMMY PATH — deploy.js writes blank.sqlite here. After deploy, patch server/.env and server/mcp/mcp_server_config.json to point at ../../data/ca_schools.sqlite

product.name: Bloomsights
product.description: Bloomsights is a student behavioral health platform for K-12 schools. Students self-report their emotional wellbeing, social connectedness, and peer relationships through adaptive check-ins every 4-6 weeks. Educators get dashboards, sociograms, 16 behavioral health indicators, MTSS-aligned tiered support tools, and intervention logs. Not a survey tool — a continuous measurement system that makes the invisible visible: who's connected, who's isolated, who's struggling, and what school climate actually looks like from the student's perspective.
differentiators: Sociograms — auto-generated social maps showing isolated vs. connected students. Isolation is visible before it becomes a behavioral incident.
16 behavioral health indicators built from longitudinal student data — more reliable than any single question or annual climate survey.
MTSS-aligned: tiered intervention tools map directly to Tier 1/2/3 support workflows principals already use.
Adaptive check-ins prevent survey fatigue — questions rotate by priority, personalized per student.
Shows which students are falling through the cracks before they fall — lagging indicators (grades, attendance, discipline) are too late.
product.geography: Los Angeles metro area — K-12 schools (public, charter, private, religious)
product.pricing: Contact adam@bloomsights.com for district and school pricing.

factletTopics: student mental health K-12 Los Angeles
Jewish day school student mental health
factletNotTopics: academic test scores unrelated to wellbeing
school board politics unrelated to student health

criteria.0: No contact found or no email
criteria.1: Generic inbox (info@, office@, admin@) or role unclear
criteria.2: Named decision-maker (principal, head of school, dean) with direct email

criteria.0: No evidence of student wellbeing focus or SEL initiative
criteria.1: General wellness language on website or in bio (mentions SEL, counseling, whole child)
criteria.2: Active signal: announced program, grant, initiative, or public statement about student mental health

criteria.0: No evidence of unmet need — school appears resourced or problem not visible
criteria.1: Indirect evidence: counselor vacancy, high caseloads, vague climate language
criteria.2: Direct evidence: stated gap, incident response, grant application, public concern about student mental health

criteria.0: No useful intel (no social, no site, no news beyond directory listing)
criteria.1: Scraped something useful — program details, staff bios, news coverage, social posts

criteria.0: Only generic info — no differentiating facts about this school or person
criteria.1: At least one fact unique to THIS school or contact (named initiative, specific challenge, recent post)

criteria.0: Nothing specific enough to open with — draft would be generic
criteria.1: Clear hook exists — specific program, stated need, recent news, or school characteristic

criteria.0: Outside LA / not a K-12 school (preschool-only, tutoring center, adult ed)

hardGates: No email at all → warmthScore = 0, draftStatus = brewing, do not compose

Generic inbox only (customerservice@, info@, office@, admin@, contact@, hello@, webmaster@, support@, or any role/department address) with no verified direct personal email → warmthScore cap = 5 maximum, regardless of dossier richness. Score Contact Quality = 1. Do not round up.
Not a K-12 school (preschool-only, tutoring center, adult ed) → warmthScore = 0, mark brewing, log OUT_OF_SCOPE

evaluatorCriteria.name: Specificity
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft reference something specific to THIS school or principal — not 'schools like yours'?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: References a named program, specific challenge, recent news, or observable school characteristic unique to them
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: 'Schools today face unprecedented challenges with student mental health...' / 'Principals like you are seeing...' — generic, could go to anyone

evaluatorCriteria.name: Recency
evaluatorCriteria.description: Is the hook based on something observable or current — not just static directory data?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: References a recent announcement, news item, grant, program launch, or social post (2020 or later)
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Draft is based only on school address and name — no research visible

evaluatorCriteria.name: Pain-to-Product Bridge
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft connect a visible gap at THIS school to what Bloomsights specifically does?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: One sentence: their specific challenge (counselor overload, invisible isolation, MTSS gap) → Bloomsights fills it precisely
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Describes Bloomsights features without connecting to their stated or observable problem

evaluatorCriteria.name: Brevity
evaluatorCriteria.description: Under 150 words. Every sentence earns its place.
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: Tight, punchy, nothing wasted. No filler phrases.
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Over 150 words, or contains any forbidden phrase

evaluatorCriteria.name: Reply Test
evaluatorCriteria.description: Would a busy principal reply or forward this to their counselor or district?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: They screenshot it and send it to their dean of students
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Finger on delete by sentence two — feels like a sales blast

outreachRules.tone: direct, knowledgeable, collegial — sounds like someone who understands schools, not a vendor pitching software
outreachRules.openWith: Lead with something specific about THIS school — their program, their challenge, their students. Never open with your company name or a product pitch.
outreachRules.closeWith: Close with a concrete ask: 'Would 20 minutes this week make sense?' or 'Can I send you a one-pager?' — not 'let me know if you're interested'
forbidden: I hope this finds you well
